TypeSignature.Value: .class public auto ansi sealed beforefieldinit Comparer extends System.Object implements class System.Collections.IComparer, class System.Runtime.Serialization.ISerializable
Public NotInheritable Class Comparer Implements IComparer, ISerializable
type Comparer = class interface IComparer interface ISerializable
public ref class Comparer sealed : System::Collections::IComparer, System::Runtime::Serialization::ISerializable

Docs.summary: Compares two objects for equivalence, where string comparisons are case-sensitive.

remarks.format:
## Remarks This class is the default implementation of the <xref:System.Collections.IComparer> interface. The <xref:System.Collections.CaseInsensitiveComparer> class is the implementation of the <xref:System.Collections.IComparer> interface that performs case-insensitive string comparisons. <xref:System.Collections.Generic.Comparer`1?displayProperty=nameWithType> is the generic equivalent of this class.
Comparison procedures use the <xref:System.Threading.Thread.CurrentCulture*?displayProperty=nameWithType> of the current thread unless otherwise specified. String comparisons might have different results depending on the culture. For more information on culture-specific comparisons, see the <xref:System.Globalization> namespace and [Globalization and Localization](/dotnet/standard/globalization-localization/).
## Examples The following code example shows how <xref:System.Collections.Comparer.Compare*> returns different values depending on the culture associated with the <xref:System.Collections.Comparer>.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Collections/Comparer/Overview/comparercultures.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Collections/Comparer/Overview/comparercultures.vb" id="Snippet1":::

MemberSignature.Value: public Comparer (System.Globalization.CultureInfo culture);
.method public hidebysig specialname rtspecialname instance void .ctor(class System.Globalization.CultureInfo culture) cil managed
Public Sub New (culture As CultureInfo)
new System.Collections.Comparer : System.Globalization.CultureInfo -> System.Collections.Comparer
public: Comparer(System::Globalization::CultureInfo ^ culture);

Docs.param: The to use for the new .
Docs.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class using the specified .

remarks.format:
## Remarks Comparison procedures use the specified <xref:System.Globalization.CultureInfo?displayProperty=nameWithType> to determine the sort order and casing rules. String comparisons might have different results depending on the culture. For more information on culture-specific comparisons, see the <xref:System.Globalization> namespace and [Globalization and Localization](/dotnet/standard/globalization-localization/).
## Examples The following code example shows how <xref:System.Collections.Comparer.Compare*> returns different values depending on the culture associated with the <xref:System.Collections.Comparer>.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Collections/Comparer/Overview/comparercultures.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Collections/Comparer/Overview/comparercultures.vb" id="Snippet1":::

MemberSignature.Value: public int Compare (object? a, object? b);
.method public hidebysig newslot virtual instance int32 Compare(object a, object b) cil managed
Public Function Compare (a As Object, b As Object) As Integer
abstract member Compare : obj * obj -> int override this.Compare : obj * obj -> int
public: virtual int Compare(System::Object ^ a, System::Object ^ b);

Docs.summary: Performs a case-sensitive comparison of two objects of the same type and returns a value indicating whether one is less than, equal to, or greater than the other.
Docs.returns:
A signed integer that indicates the relative values of and , as shown in the following table.
Value
Meaning
Less than zero
is less than .
Zero
equals .
Greater than zero
is greater than .

remarks.format:
## Remarks If `a` implements <xref:System.IComparable>, then `a`. `CompareTo` (`b`) is returned; otherwise, if `b` implements <xref:System.IComparable>, then the negated result of `b`. `CompareTo` (`a`) is returned.
Comparing `null` with any type is allowed and does not generate an exception when using <xref:System.IComparable>. When sorting, `null` is considered to be less than any other object.
String comparisons might have different results depending on the culture. For more information on culture-specific comparisons, see the <xref:System.Globalization> namespace and [Globalization and Localization](/dotnet/standard/globalization-localization/).
## Examples The following code example shows how <xref:System.Collections.Comparer.Compare*> returns different values depending on the culture associated with the <xref:System.Collections.Comparer>.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Collections/Comparer/Overview/comparercultures.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Collections/Comparer/Overview/comparercultures.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Docs.exception:
Neither nor implements the interface.
-or-
and are of different types and neither one can handle comparisons with the other.

Docs.summary: Represents an instance of that is associated with the of the current thread. This field is read-only.

remarks.format: ## Remarks Comparison procedures use the <xref:System.Threading.Thread.CurrentCulture*?displayProperty=nameWithType> of the current thread to determine the sort order and casing rules. String comparisons might have different results depending on the culture. For more information on culture-specific comparisons, see the <xref:System.Globalization> namespace and [Globalization and Localization](/dotnet/standard/globalization-localization/).

Docs.summary: Represents an instance of that is associated with . This field is read-only.

remarks.format:
## Remarks Comparison procedures use the <xref:System.Globalization.CultureInfo.InvariantCulture*?displayProperty=nameWithType> to determine the sort order and casing rules. String comparisons might have different results depending on the culture. For more information on culture-specific comparisons, see the <xref:System.Globalization> namespace and [Globalization and Localization](/dotnet/standard/globalization-localization/).
## Examples The following code example shows how <xref:System.Collections.Comparer.Compare*> returns different values depending on the culture associated with the <xref:System.Collections.Comparer>.
:::code language="csharp" source="~/snippets/csharp/System.Collections/Comparer/Overview/comparercultures.cs" id="Snippet1"::: :::code language="vb" source="~/snippets/visualbasic/System.Collections/Comparer/Overview/comparercultures.vb" id="Snippet1":::

Attribute.AttributeName: [System.Obsolete("This API supports obsolete formatter-based serialization. It should not be called or extended by application code.", DiagnosticId="SYSLIB0051", UrlFormat="https://aka.ms/dotnet-warnings/{0}")]

Docs.param: The object to populate with data.
The context information about the source or destination of the serialization.
